ticker_items: Iran halts Strait of Hormuz traffic after US-Israeli strikes
New Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured in airstrikes
Israeli forces continue targeting Hezbollah strongholds in Beirut
Indian ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz amid escalating tensions
Pentagon burns billions as Iran war enters second week

new_entrant.text: Australia deployed troops to the Middle East, with senators warning the country is helping the US in the Iran conflict.

middle_east.description: IDF executed precision airstrikes across Beirut targeting Hezbollah strongholds.
Iranian forces attacked Indian commercial ship bound for Kandla port.
US Embassy in Riyadh hit by two Iranian drones causing limited fire and minor damage.

observers.description: India condemned Iranian attacks on its commercial vessels for the first time. Defense stocks are rising on expectations of increased military spending due to regional tensions.
Australia has deployed troops to the Middle East to assist US operations. Senator Nick McKim warned that Prime Minister Albanese has hitched Australia to the conflict.
South Africa is navigating an awkward diplomatic dance between Washington and Tehran. Pretoria is attempting to maintain relations with both sides of the conflict.
China is strengthening ties with North Korea amid the Middle East crisis. New infrastructure projects are underway along the China-North Korea border to boost trade and influence.

financial.notes: Oil prices are rising due to Iran halting traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, while Trump announced releasing Strategic Petroleum Reserves to ease market pressure.

update_log.text: Iranian drones struck US Embassy in Riyadh causing minor damage
Israel continued precision airstrikes on Hezbollah positions in Beirut
Iran attacked Indian commercial ship in Strait of Hormuz
Reports emerged of Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei being injured in initial airstrikes
